title: LLM Provider Abstraction Layer
subtitle: LLM Provider Abstraction Layer
description: Agent systems need to support multiple LLM providers (OpenAI/Anthropic/local models) with runtime capability detection and unified error handling. This domain addresses how to design a provider abstraction layer that decouples upper-layer Agent code from specific LLM implementations.
sub_problems: Model capability detection (function calling, vision, etc.)
Unified error mapping and exception handling
Thinking tags cleanup (reasoning model <think> tag filtering)
best_practices: Use registry + factory pattern to manage provider lifecycle
Implement capabilities detection layer to enable Agent dynamic behavior adjustment based on model capabilities
Use MODEL_OVERRIDES for model-level exceptions (e.g., forced_temperature) to avoid provider-level configuration bloat
Error mapping adopts optional SDK loading (try import), rules for uninstalled SDKs are automatically skipped
Stream retry requires manual loop implementation, tenacity does not support AsyncGenerator decoration

values.DeepTutor: Static configuration table PROVIDER_CAPABILITIES × MODEL_OVERRIDES with 4-level fallback
MappingRule chain + status_code fast path with optional SDK loading
URL blacklist/whitelist auto-routing via CLOUD_DOMAINS + LOCAL_HOSTS + LOCAL_PORTS
Two-layer retry: built-in BaseLLMProvider + Factory tenacity with circuit breaker pre-check
Independent adapter pattern with EmbeddingProviderManager static mapping of 6 bindings
Per-provider independent TrafficController + global ErrorRateTracker sliding window

solutions.title: Abstraction
solutions.description: Unify LLM/embedding multi-provider interface differences with capability detection, error mapping, and intelligent routing
